programme.title: FishON | River Run Redemption
programme.desc: Paul and Perry kick off an adventurous journey down the Ivanhoe River, heading toward a lake that’s known to deliver steady walleye action. But this time, the fish had other plans. With walleye proving tough to track down, the duo turns to the river’s reliable residents—Northern Pike—to keep the rods bending and the spirits high. Surrounded by stunning scenery, flowing rapids, and beautiful waterfalls, this river run becomes more than just a fishing trip—it’s a reminder that the North’s wild beauty always makes the day worthwhile, no matter what’s biting.

programme.title: Travel Quest Ep105: Yap
programme.desc: This remote Pacific island is a diver’s paradise and we take advantage of this by diving with giant Manta Rays the size of a small plane. The craziest dive though is when Bill feeds dozens of sharks with no protective gear. We also film where no one has filmed before…The Forbidden Island.

programme.title: Shark Dive TV Ep111: Sand Tiger Sharks as Protectors
programme.desc: Shark Dive TV demonstrates how Sand Tiger Sharks act as protectors and takes a Deeper Dive by explaining male vs female shark differences, shark reproduction, Gray Reef Sharks and shark buoyancy.

programme.title: World of Watersports Ep315: Cliff Diving, Wing Foiling and Sailing
programme.desc: World of Watersports explores the dangerous sport of Cliff Diving and profiles Gary Hunt - one of the very best cliff divers on the planet. In addition, WOW features wing foiling in Switzerland and Bacardi Cup Sailing in Bermuda.

programme.title: Blue World Ep606: The Amazing Comeback of Gray Seals + Shark Performances in the Philippines
programme.desc: Gray seals look like cute swimming puppy dogs, and their story of recovery from the brink of extinction is one of history's best examples of conservation. Then we travel to the town of Oslob in the Philippines, where the locals have switched from hunting Whale Sharks to teaching them to perform for tourists.

programme.title: Xplash Ep111
programme.desc: The Xplash action is intense as we kayak Africa's Zambezi River, go behind-the-scenes of Australia's V8 Powerboat Racing, and head north to the icy waters of Canada and Alaska for coldwater surfing.

programme.title: Boom America
programme.desc: Kevin Harrington and Toli Cefail meet with the team at Advanced Vitality to explore the cutting edge of preventative healthcare. With over 60% of Americans facing accelerated cellular aging, the company is looking to scale fast—and needs the right strategy to do it. Next, Cindy from Scout & Zoe’s joins the conversation to share how her insect-based dog treats are creating buzz in the pet industry. Her clever and down-to-earth grassroots marketing approach (pun intended) is changing the game for alternative protein sources. Finally, Kevin and Toli sit down with leaders from Hammer Haag Steel and Zenith Trek to dive into the challenges and opportunities of building complex, large-scale equipment and structures.

programme.title: Boom America
programme.desc: The A-Team gets back to work—this time diving into cross-marketing strategies for Advanced Vitality. Over at Hammer Haag, the team discusses launching a gritty new “Micro Dirty Jobs” miniseries to spotlight American manufacturing in action. We’re also introduced to serial entrepreneur Jeff Kline, founder of 1-800-Medigap, who brings a powerhouse presence to the team’s growing roster. Meanwhile, Cindy from Scout & Zoe’s returns for a full brand overhaul with the experts. Newest A-Team member Rudy Mawer steps in with high-level influencer insight and a mission to empower the next generation of entrepreneurs. And finally, Zenith Trek makes bold moves to break into big box retail—with the A-Team guiding the way.

programme.title: Like a Train
programme.desc: Overconfidence can doom a season and the Knights meet their biggest obstacle of the year. Were they ready or not? Why was Coach Jesse so upset on Halloween night?

programme.title: Country Ball
programme.desc: Meet Kayleb Wagner, Florida’s all-time record-breaking running back from a football recruitment powerhouse state. On September 17, 2021, Kayleb shattered the state rushing record previously held by Derrick Henry—Alabama alum, now a Tennessee Titan and one of the NFL’s most dominant running backs. As a new superstar flooded with media attention, Kayleb was poised for scholarship offers, including one from Army that was later withdrawn. The reason? Despite his athletic and academic excellence, Kayleb has one arm. Join Country Ball to hear Kayleb’s journey through his own words and those of his inspiring mom, Sam, exploring where he’s been, what he’s doing now, and where he’s headed.

programme.title: Country Ball
programme.desc: The first part of the season finale starts now. Jesse’s back on his home turf of Clearwater, Florida, at Clearwater Academy International with not one, but TWO of his top players: Luca Stanzani and Dylan Djeté – who’ve both been playing football since they were little. Dylan’s a family-oriented player who got five touchdowns across his first two games in America. Luca’s been CAI’s starting quarterback since eighth grade and has both the brains and the dedication to go far. See just what these players can do in this episode of Country Ball!

programme.title: Inside Pro Surfing: Rip Curl Pro Bells Beach presented by Bonsoy 2025
programme.desc: Bells Beach on Victoria's South Coast is the longest-running surfing competition and will host the 62st edition this year. Bells is Australian surfing heritage. With the hope of scoring long-period, groomed walls, the surfers will need to display the perfect combination of power, precision, and timing. The biggest surfing legends have had the honor of winning and ringing ‘The Bell' and 2025 will see a hungry new generation vying for the opportunity to cement their names in one of surfing's most prestigious events.

programme.title: African Renaissance | Brilliant Corners: Senegal, Episode 3
programme.desc: Sam, Easkey, and Adrien catch up with West African surf legend Oumar Seye, the first West African sponsored surfer. Seye has impacted the surf culture in Senegal in more ways than one, both proving the sport can be more than a hobby and supporting sustainability by banning plastic bags at his surf events. They end their trip in Popenguine, a bohemian beach town where an incredible women-led initiative to plant thousands of trees has made the area one of the greenest parts of the country.
